_comment: WC2026 Group I, MD3. Standings before: France 6, Norway 6, Senegal 0 (1-3 France, 1-3 Norway), Iraq 0 (1-4 Norway, 1-3 France). CORRECTION: Senegal is NOT eliminated -- a win takes them to 3 pts and they can still go through as one of the 8 BEST THIRDS. Crucially, the best-thirds tiebreak is on GOAL DIFFERENCE / goals, so Senegal is motivated not just to win but to win BY A MARGIN. This is a legitimate STRUCTURAL incentive (qualification + GD matters), NOT a speculative motivation tweak -- so unlike the dead-rubber framing, here we DO lift Senegal (clearer favourite, goal-hungry, open game). Iraq are effectively out (GD -6 makes a best-third near-impossible) so they play more for pride, but no over-downgrade (South Africa lesson). Home = Senegal, away = Iraq. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Senegal (0 pts) is NOT eliminated: a win -- ideally by a margin -- can sneak them through as a best third (the spot is decided on goal difference). Iraq (0 pts, GD -6) are effectively out. Senegal motivated to attack and run up the score.

home.name: Senegal
notes: Quality side that underperformed; now PLAYING TO QUALIFY as a best third and the GD matters -> they will push for goals. attack 1.28, context 1.05 (real structural incentive, not a guess).
Decent defensively vs a weak Iraq. defense 0.98.
Clearer favourite and goal-hungry -> open game, rho -0.05.

away.name: Iraq
notes: Leaky, and Senegal will attack -> higher Iraq-conceded. defense 1.12.
Effectively out (GD -6); pride only, but no over-downgrade.
